questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education are required for a Louisiana salesperson?
questions.explanation: Louisiana requires 90 hours of pre-license education for salesperson candidates. Broker candidates must complete 150 hours. These courses must be taken through LREC-approved education providers.

questions.question_text: A new salesperson receives her initial license on March 1. By what date must she complete post-license education?
distractors: By December 31 of the same year
Within one year of initial licensure
questions.explanation: Louisiana requires 45 hours of post-license education within 180 days of the initial license date. For a March 1 license, the deadline would be approximately August 28. Failure to complete post-license on time can jeopardize the license.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education does Louisiana require for new salespersons?
questions.explanation: New Louisiana salespersons must complete 45 hours of post-license education within 180 days of receiving their initial license. These hours may count toward annual CE requirements but cannot satisfy mandatory topic requirements.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson completes 45 hours of post-license education. Can these hours also satisfy her annual continuing education requirement?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, but not the mandatory topic hours
questions.explanation: Post-license hours may count toward the annual CE requirement. However, they cannot substitute for the mandatory topic(s) designated by LREC each year. The licensee must still complete the required mandatory topic hours separately.

questions.question_text: What is the difference in pre-license education hours between a Louisiana broker candidate and a salesperson candidate?
questions.explanation: Salesperson candidates need 90 hours and broker candidates need 150 hours, a difference of 60 hours. The additional broker education covers advanced topics in brokerage management, trust accounts, and supervision.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must Louisiana licensees complete annually?
questions.explanation: Louisiana requires 12 hours of continuing education annually. This must include mandatory topic(s) designated by LREC for that year. All hours must be completed by December 31.

questions.question_text: By what date must Louisiana licensees complete their annual continuing education?
questions.explanation: All 12 hours of annual continuing education must be completed by December 31 each year. Failure to complete CE on time results in a fine citation from LREC.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes 12 hours of elective CE courses but skips the LREC mandatory topic. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: The CE requirement is not satisfied
distractors: Elective hours substitute for the topic
questions.explanation: Annual CE must include the mandatory topic(s) designated by LREC. Completing only elective hours without the mandatory topic does not satisfy the requirement, even if the total reaches 12 hours. The licensee faces a fine citation.

questions.question_text: What penalty does LREC impose on a licensee who fails to complete annual continuing education by the deadline?
questions.explanation: Louisiana imposes a fine citation on licensees who fail to complete their 12 hours of CE, including mandatory topics, by December 31. This is an administrative penalty; continued noncompliance could lead to further disciplinary action.

questions.question_text: LREC designates a new mandatory CE topic on fair housing updates for the current year. A licensee has already completed 10 hours of general CE. What must she still do?
questions.correct_answer: Complete the mandatory topic plus 2 hours
distractors: Retake all 12 hours including the topic
questions.explanation: The licensee needs 12 total hours including the mandatory topic. Her 10 general hours count toward the total, but she must still complete the mandatory topic. If the mandatory topic is 2 or more hours, she completes it to reach the total requirement.

questions.question_text: Under Act 193 of 2021, the Louisiana real estate license year runs from when to when?
questions.explanation: Act 193 of 2021 established a calendar year license cycle running January 1 through December 31. This standardized the renewal period for all Louisiana real estate licensees.

questions.question_text: During which period can Louisiana licensees submit timely renewal applications?
questions.explanation: The timely renewal window is August 1 through September 30. Renewals submitted after September 30 incur delinquent fees. The license remains valid through December 31 of the calendar year.

questions.question_text: A licensee renews her license on October 15. What additional cost will she incur?
questions.explanation: The timely renewal period ends September 30. Renewals after that date are considered delinquent and subject to additional delinquent fees. The license remains valid through December 31 but the late fee applies.

questions.question_text: What are the minimum E&O insurance coverage limits required for Louisiana licensees?
questions.explanation: Louisiana mandates errors and omissions insurance with minimum limits of $100,000 per occurrence and $300,000 aggregate. The maximum allowable deductible is $1,000. This coverage protects consumers from licensee negligence.

questions.question_text: A broker is shopping for E&O insurance policies. One policy offers a $2,500 deductible at a lower premium. Can the broker select this policy to meet LREC requirements?
distractors: Yes, any deductible amount is permitted
Yes, if the premium is paid in full
No, E&O policies cannot have deductibles
questions.explanation: Louisiana requires E&O insurance with a maximum deductible of $1,000. A $2,500 deductible exceeds the LREC limit and would not satisfy the mandatory insurance requirement, regardless of premium savings.
